steps.title: Identify what the underlined portion says and where it sits
steps.content: The underlined portion states what researchers previously believed: Denisovans lived only at high altitudes in cold climates in Russia and China. It appears just before the word but, which signals contrast. What follows the “but” is the new discovery — a molar found in Laos suggesting Denisovans also lived at low altitudes in warm climates. The underlined portion is therefore setting up a baseline so the discovery’s importance can register.

steps.title: Match the function to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It dismisses as untrue the research presented in the previous sentence.’: The underlined portion describes prior beliefs; it doesn’t dismiss any research as false. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It defines a term used in the description that follows in the rest of the sentence.’: No term is being defined here. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It emphasizes the main goal of the research introduced in the previous sentence.’: The text states what the research discovered, not what its goal was. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It provides context that clarifies the significance of the information that follows in the rest of the sentence.’: By showing how limited the prior understanding was (high-altitude, cold-climate only), the underlined portion makes the Laos discovery — low-altitude, warm-climate — significantly more striking. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
